agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Critical SQL injection vulnerability: unsanitized user input directly interpolated into SQL query from public-facing API."
  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "SQL Injection via f-string query construction",
      "risk": "An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through customer_id or status_filter, enabling full database read/write/delete, authentication bypass, or remote code execution depending on DB engine.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — query construction on lines 2-6",
      "reproduction": "Call the endpoint with status_filter = \"delivered' OR '1'='1\" to dump all deliveries for all customers. Call with customer_id = \"'; DROP TABLE deliveries; --\" to destroy data.",
      "fix": "Use parameterized queries: db_conn.execute(\"SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?\", (customer_id, status_filter)). If using psycopg2 use %s placeholders; for SQLAlchemy use :param bound parameters. Never use f-strings or string concatenation for SQL.",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security: all endpoints accepting user input must use parameterized queries"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on public API parameters",
      "risk": "Even with parameterized queries, unbounded or untyped inputs allow logic abuse — e.g., querying arbitrary customer_ids (IDOR), or passing unexpected status values to probe internal states.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — customer_id and status_filter parameters",
      "reproduction": "Pass another user's customer_id to retrieve their deliveries. Pass internal status values like 'cancelled_fraud' to probe order states not intended for external consumers.",
      "fix": "Validate customer_id matches the authenticated user's identity (authz check). Validate status_filter against an allowlist of permitted statuses (e.g., {'pending', 'shipped', 'delivered'}). Return 400/403 for invalid values.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Authorization: users must only access their own data; input validation on all public endpoints"
    },
    {
      "title": "SELECT * returns unbounded result set with no pagination",
      "risk": "A customer with thousands of deliveries (or an attacker exploiting the injection) can trigger a full table scan, causing memory exhaustion or DB connection starvation under load.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — query and fetchall()",
      "reproduction": "Query a customer with 500k+ deliveries, or exploit injection to remove the WHERE clause entirely. fetchall() loads every row into memory.",
      "fix": "Add LIMIT/OFFSET pagination (e.g., LIMIT 100). Replace SELECT * with explicit column list. Consider using fetchmany() or server-side cursors for large results. Add a query timeout on the DB connection.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Performance: API must handle high-volume customers without degradation"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No error handling for DB failures",
      "description": "If the query fails (connection timeout, syntax error, constraint violation), the raw exception propagates to the caller. In a public API this leaks internal schema details in error messages.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — db_conn.execute(query)"
    },
    {
      "title": "SELECT * couples API response to schema changes",
      "description": "Adding internal columns (e.g., internal_notes, fraud_score) to the deliveries table automatically exposes them to external consumers. Use explicit column selection.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — SELECT *"
    }
  ],
  "suggestions": [
    "Wrap DB calls in try/except and return a generic error to the caller while logging the full exception internally.",
    "Add an index on (customer_id, status) if one does not exist — this query will be the hot path.",
    "Consider using an ORM or query builder (SQLAlchemy) to enforce parameterization by default across all endpoints."
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "This code is exploitable immediately upon deployment. Any internet-facing exposure allows full database compromise via SQL injection.",
    "rollback_capability": "Rollback is safe but does not remediate — if data was exfiltrated or deleted during exposure, rollback alone is insufficient.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Switching to parameterized queries is backward-compatible for legitimate callers. Adding input validation may reject previously-accepted malformed inputs.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging of queries or failed attempts. Add audit logging for access patterns and failed validation to detect exploitation.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": ["auth_security: endpoint lacks authorization checks, allowing cross-customer data access (IDOR)"]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert deployment and take the endpoint offline. Audit DB logs for signs of injection exploitation during any exposure window."
  },
  "escalations": [],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": ["SQL injection is unambiguous and trivially exploitable", "Missing input validation and authorization are clear from the code", "Unbounded SELECT * with fetchall() is a measurable resource risk"],
    "low_confidence_areas": ["DB engine specifics (Postgres vs SQLite vs MySQL) affect injection payload severity but not the core vulnerability"],
    "assumptions": ["db_conn follows Python DB-API 2.0 or similar interface", "customer_id and status_filter arrive unsanitized from the HTTP layer as stated"]
  }
}

content.thinking:
Reviewing this code for security and quality issues.

Clear SQL injection vulnerability - both parameters are directly interpolated into the query string from HTTP request parameters. This is a critical security issue.

No need to check files on disk - this is inline code review.

Classification: `none` - local code issue I can fully assess.

content.text:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Critical SQL injection vulnerability: unsanitized user input directly interpolated into SQL query from public-facing API."
  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "SQL Injection via f-string query construction",
      "risk": "An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through customer_id or status_filter, enabling full database read/write/delete, authentication bypass, or remote code execution depending on DB engine.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — query construction on lines 2-6",
      "reproduction": "Call the endpoint with status_filter = \"delivered' OR '1'='1\" to dump all deliveries for all customers. Call with customer_id = \"'; DROP TABLE deliveries; --\" to destroy data.",
      "fix": "Use parameterized queries: db_conn.execute(\"SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?\", (customer_id, status_filter)). If using psycopg2 use %s placeholders; for SQLAlchemy use :param bound parameters. Never use f-strings or string concatenation for SQL.",
      "time_to_fix": "15 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Security: all endpoints accepting user input must use parameterized queries"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on public API parameters",
      "risk": "Even with parameterized queries, unbounded or untyped inputs allow logic abuse — e.g., querying arbitrary customer_ids (IDOR), or passing unexpected status values to probe internal states.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — customer_id and status_filter parameters",
      "reproduction": "Pass another user's customer_id to retrieve their deliveries. Pass internal status values like 'cancelled_fraud' to probe order states not intended for external consumers.",
      "fix": "Validate customer_id matches the authenticated user's identity (authz check). Validate status_filter against an allowlist of permitted statuses (e.g., {'pending', 'shipped', 'delivered'}). Return 400/403 for invalid values.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Authorization: users must only access their own data; input validation on all public endpoints"
    },
    {
      "title": "SELECT * returns unbounded result set with no pagination",
      "risk": "A customer with thousands of deliveries (or an attacker exploiting the injection) can trigger a full table scan, causing memory exhaustion or DB connection starvation under load.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — query and fetchall()",
      "reproduction": "Query a customer with 500k+ deliveries, or exploit injection to remove the WHERE clause entirely. fetchall() loads every row into memory.",
      "fix": "Add LIMIT/OFFSET pagination (e.g., LIMIT 100). Replace SELECT * with explicit column list. Consider using fetchmany() or server-side cursors for large results. Add a query timeout on the DB connection.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Performance: API must handle high-volume customers without degradation"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No error handling for DB failures",
      "description": "If the query fails (connection timeout, syntax error, constraint violation), the raw exception propagates to the caller. In a public API this leaks internal schema details in error messages.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — db_conn.execute(query)"
    },
    {
      "title": "SELECT * couples API response to schema changes",
      "description": "Adding internal columns (e.g., internal_notes, fraud_score) to the deliveries table automatically exposes them to external consumers. Use explicit column selection.",
      "location": "search_deliveries() — SELECT *"
    }
  ],
  "suggestions": [
    "Wrap DB calls in try/except and return a generic error to the caller while logging the full exception internally.",
    "Add an index on (customer_id, status) if one does not exist — this query will be the hot path.",
    "Consider using an ORM or query builder (SQLAlchemy) to enforce parameterization by default across all endpoints."
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "This code is exploitable immediately upon deployment. Any internet-facing exposure allows full database compromise via SQL injection.",
    "rollback_capability": "Rollback is safe but does not remediate — if data was exfiltrated or deleted during exposure, rollback alone is insufficient.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Switching to parameterized queries is backward-compatible for legitimate callers. Adding input validation may reject previously-accepted malformed inputs.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No logging of queries or failed attempts. Add audit logging for access patterns and failed validation to detect exploitation.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": true,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": ["auth_security: endpoint lacks authorization checks, allowing cross-customer data access (IDOR)"]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert deployment and take the endpoint offline. Audit DB logs for signs of injection exploitation during any exposure window."
  },
  "escalations": [],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": ["SQL injection is unambiguous and trivially exploitable", "Missing input validation and authorization are clear from the code", "Unbounded SELECT * with fetchall() is a measurable resource risk"],
    "low_confidence_areas": ["DB engine specifics (Postgres vs SQLite vs MySQL) affect injection payload severity but not the core vulnerability"],
    "assumptions": ["db_conn follows Python DB-API 2.0 or similar interface", "customer_id and status_filter arrive unsanitized from the HTTP layer as stated"]
  }
}
